---
title: "🟠 Pricing & Demand"
subtitle: "Price elasticity, revenue optimization, and the causal experiments behind pricing decisions."
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
page-layout: full
listing:
  - id: pricing-posts
    contents:
      - "../posts/**/*.qmd"
    include:
      categories: ["pricing", "pricing and demand", "pricing & demand"]
    sort: "date asc"
    type: default
    fields: [date, title, description, reading-time]
    max-items: 30
---

Pricing is where causal inference stops being an academic exercise and
starts moving money. Every price change is a treatment. Every demand
response is a causal effect. The methods on this page turn that
intuition into decisions defensible in front of a commercial team.

This arc reuses the vocabulary of the causal-inference arc and applies
it to elasticity, demand, revenue, and marketplace dynamics.

---

## Posts in this series

::: {#pricing-posts}
:::

---
